title: erica perspicua subsp. perspicua
intro: Erica perspicua subsp. perspicua, also known as the Bell Heather or Summer Heath, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to moorlands in the UK, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and flowers, which contain flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Erica perspicua subsp. perspicua are believed to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions of the leaves and flowers, which can be used to treat digestive issues, coughs, and colds, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
